name: Family Housing Advisory Services, Incorporate
description_short: Non-profit housing and financial counseling organization providing free homebuyer education, financial literacy, tenant rights training, and tax preparation services to low- to moderate-income families in Omaha.
description_long:
Family Housing Advisory Services, Inc. (FHAS) is a community-based non-profit organization headquartered in Omaha, Nebraska, dedicated to improving housing stability and eliminating poverty through comprehensive advisory services. The organization operates with a mission to empower families and individuals by providing accessible, free financial and housing guidance. FHAS has established itself as a trusted resource for underserved populations seeking to build wealth and achieve housing security.

FHAS offers a broad range of free services including homeownership education workshops with one-on-one personalized coaching, financial education programs focused on independence and goal achievement, tenant services designed to prevent eviction and educate renters on their rights, and free tax preparation through the Omaha EITC Coalition. Additionally, the organization provides mortgage assistance, rent and utility assistance, matched savings accounts for first-time savers, and foreclosure prevention support. All services are designed to strengthen households and communities through education and direct financial support.

What distinguishes FHAS is its holistic approach to financial empowerment that combines immediate assistance (rent, utilities, mortgage help) with long-term wealth-building education. The organization emphasizes first-time homebuyer support and targets families lacking financial literacy or homeownership examples—populations often underserved by traditional financial institutions. Their integration of tax preparation services through an EITC coalition and matched savings programs demonstrates a commitment to multi-faceted poverty reduction rather than single-issue problem solving.

FHAS is a legitimate non-profit free-help organization appropriate for low-income families seeking housing and financial guidance without cost. The website displays limited quantitative impact data (metrics show "0" across categories), which may indicate either a new organization, website display issues, or insufficient documentation of outcomes. Clients should verify current service availability and geographic service area before contacting, as the website does not clearly specify whether services are limited to Omaha or serve a broader region.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for homebuyer education, financial counseling, or tax preparation
Comprehensive service suite covering homeownership, tenant rights, financial education, and tax preparation in one organization
One-on-one personalized coaching available for homebuyer education, not just group workshops
Matched savings programs that help first-time savers build wealth through dollar-matching funds
Free EITC tax preparation through partnership with Omaha EITC Coalition, returning federal refunds to families
Direct financial assistance programs including rent, utilities, and mortgage assistance for qualifying families
Foreclosure prevention support services specifically designed to help families avoid losing homes
cons: Website impact metrics display all zeros, making it impossible to verify current scale of operations or program effectiveness
Geographic service area not clearly defined—unclear whether services are limited to Omaha or serve surrounding regions
Limited contact information and intake process documentation visible on website
No information about eligibility requirements, income thresholds, or application procedures
Website does not specify wait times, appointment availability, or how to access services
best_for: First-time homebuyers from low- to moderate-income households seeking education and personalized guidance
Renters at risk of eviction needing tenant rights education and emergency rental assistance
Low-income families entitled to EITC seeking free, professional tax preparation and refund optimization
Families seeking matched savings programs to build emergency funds or down payments for homeownership
diagnosis: FHAS is best suited for low- to moderate-income Omaha-area families seeking free housing and financial guidance, particularly first-time homebuyers and renters facing instability. The main caveat is the inability to verify current operational capacity due to missing impact data on the website and unclear service area boundaries—prospective clients should contact directly to confirm eligibility and availability.
services: One-on-one homebuyer education and personalized coaching
Financial education programs focused on independence and goal achievement
Tenant services and renter rights education
Matched savings accounts for first-time savers
Homeownership wealth-building support through equity education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
